design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate horizontally scrolling levels while dodging enemy fire and obstacles, collecting power-ups to upgrade weapon systems, and defeating progressively complex enemy formations and bosses
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Tight spatial navigation under constant threat; pattern recognition and memorization of enemy placements; resource scarcity (one life per level attempt)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Piloting an advanced military spacecraft through deep space, acquiring supercharged weapons, and confronting alien threats inspired by retro sci-fi aesthetics
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Uncompromising difficulty-first design philosophy that rejects checkpoints within levels; death penalty forces complete level restart, emphasizing memorization and execution mastery as core skill gates. Embraces arcade-era philosophy on home console platform.

difficulty.lives_system: Single-life-per-level system; dying returns player to level start with no checkpoints mid-stage
difficulty.checkpointing: No checkpoints within levels; complete restart on death is core mechanical feature and primary difficulty vector
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited; difficulty is fixed and non-negotiable. Slowdown when many sprites on-screen unintentionally aids player by making obstacles easier to visually track and dodge, creating emergent difficulty modulation

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon pickup system; players collect supercharged weapon power-ups during level progression to enhance firepower and capabilities
progression.unlock_mechanism: Linear stage progression; borrows four stages from R-Type II and adds three new original stages
replayability_hooks: High execution barrier encourages replays to master level patterns
Weapon variety creates different playstyle approaches per run
Memorization-heavy design rewards players who internalize enemy placements

world_building_techniques: Alien enemy designs inspired by classic pulp sci-fi magazines of the 1930s
22nd-century sci-fi setting provides loose thematic context
Detailed visual illustrations of extraordinarily detailed alien creatures establish tone and world aesthetic

faction_indicators: Human military (player faction, implied through spacecraft operation)
Alien invaders (antagonist faction, visually characterized as retro-pulp sci-fi entities)
danger_signals: Relentless enemy fire patterns requiring constant evasion
Alien creature designs suggesting existential threat
Environmental hazards and obstacles implying hostile territory
environmental_storytelling: Deep cosmos setting conveys isolation and scale of conflict
Progressive alien designs escalate threat perception through visual complexity
Visual aesthetic rooted in 1930s pulp sci-fi magazines establishes alternate retro-futurism tone
